title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

description: Experience the true essence of Indian cuisine with our Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. Marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, this chicken is slow-cooked over hot coals to achieve an irresistible smoky flavor and tender, juicy texture. Perfect for a memorable dinner, it brings the authentic taste of the tandoor right to your backyard.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, skin removed, deep slits made
hung curd or Greek yogurt, thick

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken for First Marinade
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep slits all over the chicken pieces, ensuring they penetrate the flesh for better flavor absorption. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with lemon juice and salt. Rub well into the slits. Let it rest for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, black pepper powder, mustard oil, and optional red food color. Mix thoroughly until you have a smooth, vibrant paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade into the second marinade mixture. Ensure every piece is generously coated. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to deeply infuse.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a two-zone fire, with one side hot and the other medium. Ensure the coals are glowing red with a light ash coating. Clean the grill grates thoroughly and brush with a little oil to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium heat zone of the grill. Cook for 10-12 minutes, turning frequently to ensure even cooking and prevent burning. Move chicken to the hotter side for a few minutes if you want more charring, but watch carefully.

instructions.title: Baste and Finish Grilling
instructions.text: Brush the chicken pieces generously with ghee or melted butter. Continue grilling, turning and basting every few minutes, until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and has beautiful charred spots. An internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) should be reached. This final grilling and basting process typically takes another 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges, mint chutney, or sliced onions. Enjoy this smoky, authentic Indian delight!

tips: For truly authentic flavor, ensure your yogurt is very thick (hung curd) to prevent the marinade from running off the chicken. You can make hung curd by draining regular yogurt in a muslin cloth for a few hours.
Do not overcrowd the grill. Cook the chicken in batches if necessary to maintain consistent grill temperature and ensure even cooking and charring.
Always let the chicken rest for a few minutes after grilling. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in more tender and flavorful meat.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the vibrant streets and bustling kitchens of India with a dish that tantalizes the senses and ignites the palate: Tandoori Chicken. While often associated with the traditional clay tandoor oven, the magic of its smoky, succulent flavor can be perfectly replicated in your backyard using a charcoal grill. This guide will walk you through creating an authentic and unforgettable Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, transforming your dinner into an exotic Indian feast.

The allure of Tandoori Chicken lies in its distinctive marinade and the unique cooking method that imparts a beautiful char and deep smoky notes. As an Indian dinner recipe, it stands out for its bold flavors and impressive presentation, making it a fantastic choice for family meals or entertaining guests. Get ready to master this classic and bring a taste of India right to your table.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is an iconic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in a mixture of yogurt and a potent blend of spices, then cooked over an open flame until tender, juicy, and beautifully charred. Traditionally, it's cooked in a tandoor, a cylindrical clay oven that reaches extremely high temperatures, searing the chicken quickly while keeping it moist inside. Replicating this experience on a charcoal grill brings a similar intense heat and, crucially, that irreplaceable smoky flavor that an oven simply cannot provide. The yogurt in the marinade acts as a tenderizer, breaking down the chicken fibers, while the spices penetrate deeply, infusing every bite with warmth and complexity. The result is a vibrant red or orange-hued chicken (thanks to Kashmiri chili powder or food coloring, if used) that is incredibly flavorful and aromatic. It’s a testament to how simple ingredients, when combined with the right cooking technique, can create something truly extraordinary.

## Ingredients You Need for Tandoori Chicken

To create the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, you'll need a combination of fresh, high-quality ingredients.

For the Chicken:
*   2-3 pounds bone-in, skinless chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a mix are ideal; breasts can be used but may dry out faster). Score the chicken pieces deeply with a knife to allow the marinade to penetrate.

For the First Marinade:
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
*   1 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (or cayenne pepper)

For the Second Marinade (Yogurt-based):
*   1 cup thick plain yogurt (Greek yogurt or hung curd works best)
*   2 tablespoons Tandoori masala powder
*   1 tablespoon Kashmiri chili powder (for vibrant red color and mild heat; adjust to taste)
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1 tablespoon mustard oil (optional, but adds an authentic pungent note and helps with charring)
*   Pinch of red food color (optional, for intense restaurant-style color)

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Lemon wedges

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Charcoal Grilling

Mastering the art of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is incredibly rewarding. Follow these steps for a perfect outcome:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep slits on each piece (2-3 slits per drumstick/thigh) to help the marinade penetrate.
2.  **First Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, salt, and red chili powder. Rub this mixture all over the chicken pieces, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator. This initial marinade tenderizes the chicken and builds a flavor base.
3.  **Second Marinade:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick yogurt, Tandoori masala, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, black pepper, garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, and mustard oil (if using). If you're using food color, mix it in now.
4.  **Combine Marination:** Add the chicken from the first marinade to the second yogurt mixture. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 6 hours, or preferably overnight (up to 24 hours). The longer it marinates, the more flavorful and tender your Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken will be.
5.  **Prepare the Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create two heat zones: a direct heat zone (coals directly underneath the grates) and an indirect heat zone (no coals underneath, for slower cooking). Aim for a medium-high heat, around 375-400°F (190-200°C). Clean and oil your grill grates to prevent sticking.
6.  **Grilling Process:**
    *   Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the hot coals (direct heat zone) for 2-3 minutes per side, just until they develop a nice char and color. Be careful not to burn them due to the yogurt marinade.
    *   Once charred, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone. Close the grill lid and continue to cook, turning occasionally, for about 20-30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) in the thickest part of the chicken.
    *   During the last 5-10 minutes, you can baste the chicken with a little melted butter or oil for extra moisture and shine.
7.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring moist and tender results. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination Time is Key:** Do not skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken soaks in the marinade, the deeper the flavors will penetrate, and the more tender the meat will become. Overnight is truly best.
*   **Thick Yogurt Matters:** Use Greek yogurt or drain regular yogurt through a cheesecloth for an hour or two to remove excess whey. This prevents the marinade from becoming too watery and ensures it clings well to the chicken.
*   **Don't Fear the Char:** A little char is desirable and contributes to the authentic smoky flavor. However, keep an eye on it to prevent burning.
*   **Temperature Control:** A two-zone fire is crucial. The initial high heat sears the outside, locking in juices and creating that beautiful crust, while the indirect heat allows the chicken to cook through without drying out.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and can lead to uneven cooking and steaming instead of grilling.
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Just like any grilled meat, letting the Tandoori chicken rest for a few minutes after cooking ensures the juices redistribute, resulting in a more succulent bite.
*   **Use a Thermometer:** To guarantee perfectly cooked and safe chicken, use an instant-read meat thermometer. Chicken is done when it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).

## Serving Suggestions for Tandoori Chicken

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is incredibly versatile and pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian sides, making for a complete and satisfying Indian dinner recipe.

*   **Bread:** Serve with warm naan bread, roti, or paratha to scoop up the flavorful juices and marinade.
*   **Chutney:** A cool and refreshing mint-cilantro chutney (made with fresh mint, cilantro, green chilies, yogurt, and spices) is the perfect counterpoint to the spicy chicken.
*   **Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion, and cilantro with a lemon dressing) adds freshness and crunch.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt raita (yogurt mixed with grated cucumber or boondi, and a sprinkle of roasted cumin powder) helps balance the heat.
*   **Rice:** For a heartier meal, serve alongside a fragrant basmati rice pilaf or a simple jeera rice (cumin rice).
*   **Onions:** Thinly sliced red onions, often marinated quickly in lemon juice and a pinch of salt, offer a sharp, fresh bite.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a masterpiece, you can experiment with variations to suit your taste and dietary needs:

*   **Chicken Cuts:** While bone-in chicken is recommended for flavor and moisture, you can use boneless chicken thighs or breasts. Adjust grilling time accordingly, as boneless cuts will cook faster.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder and Kashmiri chili powder to control the heat. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper. For less, reduce the chili powders.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** The Tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile! Apply it to paneer (Indian cheese), mushrooms, cauliflower florets, or chunky vegetables like bell peppers and onions, then grill them for a delicious vegetarian "Tandoori" dish.
*   **Other Proteins:** Fish or shrimp can also be marinated in a similar fashion, though their cooking times will be much shorter.
*   **Cooking Methods (if no charcoal grill):** While the smoky flavor of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is unparalleled, you can adapt this recipe for an oven (bake at 400°F/200°C, then broil for char) or a gas grill (follow similar direct/indirect heat principles). However, for that truly authentic, deep smoky taste, charcoal remains the champion.

Bringing the authentic taste of India to your home has never been easier or more delicious. This Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken recipe promises a dinner that’s rich in flavor, tender in texture, and wonderfully smoky. It's an Indian dinner recipe that's sure to impress and become a staple in your culinary repertoire. So fire up that grill, embrace the spices, and savor the incredible results!
